functions.label: isCustomSkeletonNode
description: Type guard for the `{ node }` escape-hatch variant.

interfaces.label: AssemblyFactory
description:
Factory for defining parts and parsing children within an assembly.

Created by

. Provides `definePart` for creating
typed part factories and `parseChildren` for extracting configuration
from React children.

children.label: name
description: The assembly name (literal type preserved).

children.label: definePart
description: Define a part within this assembly.
signature: <TPresetMap extends object = Record<string, unknown>, TOutput = React.ReactNode, TContext = void>(partDefinition: { name: string; }) =>

children.label: partDefinition
description: - The part definition.

children.label: name
description: - Part name (e.g., `'column'`, `'filter'`).

children.label: parseChildren
description:
Parse React children for declarative components in this assembly.

Returns a flat array in source order. Each item is either a
`ParsedPart` (with `part`, `preset`, `instanceId`, `attributes`) or
a `ParsedChild` passthrough. Use the `type` field to discriminate.

All part types in the assembly are matched in a single pass.
Filter by `item.part` to scope to a specific part type.

In development, warns for function component children that are not
registered parts. Pass `{ supportsOtherChildren: true }` to suppress
the warning when the renderer intentionally handles non-part children.
signature: (children: React.ReactNode, options?: { supportsOtherChildren?: boolean | undefined; } | undefined) =>

children.label: children
description: - React children to parse.

children.label: options
description: - Optional parsing options.

children.label: supportsOtherChildren
description: - When `true`, suppresses the

returnComment: Flat array of parsed items in source order.

interfaces.label: ParsedChild
description:
A non-part child from the React tree.

Passthrough children that are not declarative parts (e.g., plain text,
unknown components) are preserved in source order.

children.label: type
description: Discriminator. Always `'child'` for passthrough children.

children.label: node
description: The original React node.

interfaces.label: ParsedPart
description:
A parsed declarative part extracted from React children.

The `part` and `preset` fields are the names provided when calling
`definePart` and `createPreset`. The `attributes` are the component's
props as declared in JSX.

children.label: type
description: Discriminator. Always `'part'` for declarative parts.

children.label: part
description: Part name (e.g., `'column'`, `'filter'`).

children.label: preset
description: Preset name (e.g., `'name'`, `'sort'`), or `undefined` for custom parts.

children.label: instanceId
description: Unique instance identity, resolved from `props.id`, preset, or auto-generated.

children.label: attributes
description: The component's props as declared in JSX.

children.label: componentType
description:
The original component function from the React element's `type` field.

Carried through from parsing so that the resolver can look up the
per-component-instance resolver registered via `createComponent`. This
is the only way to distinguish two custom parts that share the same
part name but were created by different `createComponent` calls — the
component function reference is unique per call.

interfaces.label: PartFactory
description:
Factory for creating parts within an assembly.

Created by

. Provides methods to
create preset components, generic components, and to tag existing
components with assembly metadata.

children.label: createPreset
description:
Create a preset component. The props type is inferred from the
preset-to-props mapping provided to `definePart`.

Optionally provide a `resolve` callback that converts declarative
attributes into concrete output (e.g., an `EuiBasicTableColumn`).
The callback is stored internally and invoked by

.

Optionally provide a `skeleton` callback that describes the shape of a
loading placeholder for this preset (used by renderers to draw a
column-aware skeleton during initial load). When omitted, the renderer
falls back to its own inference rules. See
signature: <K extends keyof TPresetMap & string>(definition: { name: K; resolve?: ((attributes: TPresetMap[K], context: TContext) => TOutput | undefined) | undefined; skeleton?: ((attributes: TPresetMap[K], context: TContext) =>

children.label: definition
description: - The preset definition.

children.label: name
description: - Preset name, constrained to `keyof TPresetMap`.

children.label: resolve
description: - Optional callback to resolve attributes into output.
signature: ((attributes: TPresetMap[K], context: TContext) => TOutput | undefined) | undefined

children.label: skeleton
description: - Optional callback returning a skeleton shape

children.label: resolve
description:
Resolve a parsed part into concrete output by dispatching to the
`resolve` callback registered via `createPreset` or `createComponent`.

Checks preset resolvers first. If no preset resolver matches, falls
back to the component resolver registered via `createComponent({ resolve })`.
Returns `undefined` if no resolver handles the part.

children.label: part
description: - A parsed part from `parseChildren`.

children.label: context
description: - Context passed through to the resolve callback.

children.label: resolveSkeleton
description:
Resolve a parsed part into its skeleton shape descriptor by dispatching
to the `skeleton` callback registered via `createPreset` or
`createComponent`.

Returns `undefined` when no skeleton resolver is registered for this
preset or custom component — renderers should treat that as "fall back
to inference" rather than an error.

children.label: part
description: - A parsed part from `parseChildren`.

children.label: context
description: - Context passed through to the skeleton callback.

children.label: parseChildren
description:
Parse React children and filter to this part type.

Equivalent to calling `assembly.parseChildren(children)` and filtering
to `item.type === 'part' && item.part === thisPartName`. Use this
instead of importing the assembly factory directly.

children.label: children
description: - React children to parse.

returnComment: Parsed parts belonging to this part type, in source order.

children.label: createComponent
description:
Create a component without a preset (e.g., custom columns or custom filters).

Each call returns a **unique** `FC` reference. When a `resolve` or `skeleton`
callback is provided, it is bound exclusively to that component reference.
This means multiple `createComponent` calls for the same part — e.g. two
`RecentsFilter` instances from different history sources — each dispatch to
their own resolver independently. The resolver is looked up from the
component function carried on the parsed part (`ParsedPart.componentType`),
so there is no global shared slot that can be overwritten.
signature: (options?: { resolve?: ((attributes: P, context: TContext) => TOutput | undefined) | undefined; skeleton?: ((attributes: P, context: TContext) =>

children.label: options
description: - Optional configuration.

children.label: resolve
description: - Resolver bound to this specific component instance.
signature: ((attributes: P, context: TContext) => TOutput | undefined) | undefined

children.label: skeleton
description: - Skeleton resolver bound to this specific component instance.

children.label: tagComponent
description:
Tag an existing component with assembly metadata.

Use this when the component needs its own generic type parameter
and cannot be created via `createPreset` or `createComponent`.
signature: <C extends (...args: any[]) => null>(component: C, options?: { preset?: (keyof TPresetMap & string) | undefined; } | undefined) => C

children.label: component
description: - The component function to tag.

children.label: options
description: - Optional preset name.

returnComment: The same component, now tagged with static Symbol properties.

misc.label: DeclarativeComponent
description:
Function type for a non-generic declarative component.

Shorthand for `(props: P) => null`. This is the recommended way to type
hand-written declarative components.

misc.label: DeclarativeReturn
description:
Return type for declarative components.

Prefer

when possible. Use `DeclarativeReturn`
directly when you need to hand-write the full function signature.

misc.label: defineAssembly
description:
Define a new assembly.

Returns an

that provides `definePart()` for
creating typed part factories and `parseChildren()` for extracting
configuration from React children.

misc.label: ParsedItem
description:
A single item from the parse result -- either a declarative part or
a passthrough child. Use the `type` field to discriminate.

misc.label: SkeletonDescriptor
signature: { shape: "text"; width?: string | number | undefined; lines?:
| undefined; } | { shape: "rectangle"; width?: string | number | undefined; height?: string | number | undefined; } | { shape: "circle"; size?: string | number | undefined; }

misc.label: SkeletonOutput
description:
Result returned by a preset's `skeleton` callback.

Either a

(the common case — renderer picks the
EUI primitive) or `{ node }` as an escape hatch for exotic shapes that
can't be expressed as a single primitive (e.g. a row of icon buttons).

misc.label: SkeletonTextLines
description:
Declarative shape hint for a skeleton cell, selected from the common
loading-state primitives offered by EUI
(`EuiSkeletonText` / `EuiSkeletonRectangle` / `EuiSkeletonCircle`).

Renderers map the discriminator (`shape`) to the matching EUI component.

Permitted `lines` values for a text-shape skeleton. Mirrors EUI's
`EuiSkeletonText`'s `LineRange` so descriptors compose with the
renderer without a cast.
